product_owner.role: Primary responsibility for generating comprehensive PRDs from discovery session results
product_owner.prerequisites: - User discovery session completed - Context files generated from research - Feature scope and constraints defined

critic.role: Quality gate validator ensuring PRD meets all standards before proceeding
critic.validation_focus: - Completeness of all PRD sections - Clarity of requirements - Testability of acceptance criteria - Technical and business feasibility

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for executing this task. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read file contents for context and analysis

tool.name: MultiEdit
tool.description: Make multiple edits to a single file

tool.name: Bash
tool.description: Execute commands (e.g., gh CLI for GitHub operations)

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: Create and manage epic issues, add PRDs to GitHub

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.usage: Get up-to-date technical documentation for referenced technologies

tool.name: perplexity_mcp
tool.usage: Research market trends, competitive analysis, best practices

tool.name: exa_mcp
tool.usage: Search for industry standards, similar implementations

tool.name: code_reasoning_mcp
tool.usage: Break down complex requirements into logical components

available_tools.tool_usage_guidelines: - Use Read to examine existing project files and documentation - Use code_reasoning_mcp for complex requirement analysis - Use github_mcp to create epic issues directly in GitHub - Use context7_mcp to verify technical feasibility with current library versions - Use perplexity_mcp or exa_mcp for market research if needed - Use TodoWrite to track PRD generation progress

prompt.task: Transform the following idea into a comprehensive Product Requirements Document (PRD) using our Feature Epic Issue Template structure. Create content that follows world-class PRD best practices and integrates seamlessly with our AAFD methodology.

context_loading.instructions:
Before proceeding, load the discovery session results and generated context files:
1. Read discovery session output: - Read: {DISCOVERY_CONTEXT_PATH}/discovery-summary.md
2. Read generated context files: - Read: {DISCOVERY_CONTEXT_PATH}/business-context.md - Read: {DISCOVERY_CONTEXT_PATH}/user-research.md - Read: {DISCOVERY_CONTEXT_PATH}/competitive-analysis.md - Read: {DISCOVERY_CONTEXT_PATH}/market-trends.md
3. Read relevant project documentation based on feature domain: - For AI features: Read ai/portkey-integration.md, ai/prompt-engineering.md - For architecture decisions: Read architecture/system-design.md, architecture/service-patterns.md - For UI components: Read ui/component-patterns.md - For data/database: Read data/database-schema.md, data/supabase-patterns.md - For authentication: Read security/authentication-patterns.md - For project standards: Read CLAUDE.md for SlideHeroes-specific patterns

step.title: Load and Synthesize Discovery Results
step.description: Read all context files and discovery summary. Understand the validated user problem, business context, competitive landscape, and market trends.

step.title: Define Problem and Solution
step.description: Synthesize the discovery findings into a clear problem statement and solution approach. Reference competitive analysis and market trends to position the solution.

step.title: Establish Success Metrics
step.description: Use the business context and user research to define specific, measurable success criteria. Include user adoption, business impact, and technical performance metrics.

step.title: Document Target Users and Journey
step.description: Transform user research into detailed personas and use cases. Map the complete user journey including integration points with existing SlideHeroes features.

step.title: Specify Core Features
step.description: Define feature set based on user journey and competitive analysis. Prioritize features using discovery insights and scope recommendations.

step.title: Analyze Technical Requirements
step.description: Identify architecture impact, required technologies, and file changes. Consider technical constraints identified during discovery and market trends in technology choices.

step.title: Define Scope and Constraints
step.description: Use discovery scope recommendations and timeline constraints to clearly separate in-scope vs. out-of-scope features.

step.title: Assess Risks and Dependencies
step.description: Combine technical feasibility analysis with competitive and market risks. Include mitigation strategies based on research findings.

use_cases.use_case: As a {USER_TYPE}, I want to {ACTION} so that {BENEFIT}
As a {USER_TYPE}, I want to {ACTION} so that {BENEFIT}
As a {USER_TYPE}, I want to {ACTION} so that {BENEFIT}

step.output: Validation report with pass/fail and feedback

step.action: Refine PRD based on Critic feedback

criteria.completeness: All PRD sections filled with appropriate detail
criteria.clarity: Unambiguous requirements that developers can implement
criteria.testability: Measurable acceptance criteria for all features
criteria.alignment: Clear connection to business objectives and user needs

actions.on_fail: PRD returned to Product Owner with specific improvement requirements

context_package.business_context: Market analysis and user research insights

quality_standards.prd_checklist: - [ ] Problem clearly articulated with user impact - [ ] Success metrics are specific and measurable - [ ] Use cases follow "As a... I want... so that..." format - [ ] Acceptance criteria are testable - [ ] Technical requirements reference existing codebase patterns - [ ] File paths are accurate and categorized properly - [ ] Scope is realistic for implementation capacity - [ ] Risks have concrete mitigation strategies - [ ] All sections completed with sufficient detail - [ ] Critic Agent validation passed
